channel.title: Protein Design Podcast
channel.description: Daily automated digest of protein design, antibody engineering & enzyme design research
channel.itunes:author: Eva Dai
channel.itunes:summary: Daily automated digest of protein design, antibody engineering & enzyme design research

item.title: Daily Podcast 2026-07-05
item.description: Today’s batch of papers shows how computational thinking is reshaping three very different corners of biology and medicine, each shift nudging us to rethink what counts as a “design” problem. | If I were designing a project inspired by today’s papers I would start by marrying the structure‑guided antibody workflow from the CRP study with the FKBP4 target that emerged from the LUAD UPR signature. | A second idea builds on the nanocarrier platform and the gene signature: load siFKBP4 into the gelatin‑montmorillonite‑cerium oxide W/O/W particles and ask whether pH‑triggered release silences FKBP4 in LUAD cells.
item.itunes:author: Eva Dai
item.itunes:summary: Today’s batch of papers shows how computational thinking is reshaping three very different corners of biology and medicine, each shift nudging us to rethink what counts as a “design” problem. | If I were designing a project inspired by today’s papers I would start by marrying the structure‑guided antibody workflow from the CRP study with the FKBP4 target that emerged from the LUAD UPR signature. | A second idea builds on the nanocarrier platform and the gene signature: load siFKBP4 into the gelatin‑montmorillonite‑cerium oxide W/O/W particles and ask whether pH‑triggered release silences FKBP4 in LUAD cells.
